rulings.Case Title: PAUL O'GRADY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to the cause of action for inverse condemnation. Issues of fact, not susceptible to determination at a pleading stage, exist as to the "inherent risk, "substantial cause," and "deliberate design, contraction or maintenance" of the tree. City of Oroville v. Sup. Ct. (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1091. Motion is otherwise granted with leave to amend as to all other causes of action to allege in good faith statutory basis for liability against a public entity. = (501/CFH)

DEFENDANT WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, NOT IN ITS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY, BUT SOLELY AS TRUSTEE FOR MFRA TRUST 2014-2'S Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pending Action And For Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $1,722.50 OFF CALENDAR per request of the moving party. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAGNACHEW ALEMAYEHU ET AL VS. SERVIS ONE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2020 line 3. Preliminary Injunction The Court is inclined to grant a preliminary injunction. The discrepancy between the rental income reported and the rental income considered for loan modification determination is unexplained and is sufficient to show likelihood of Plaintiff's prevailing on the merits of the negligence-based claim; irreparable harm has also been shown. The injunction shall remain in place until Plaintiff is evaluated for a loan modification based on correct income information (either as reported or, if reported income is not considered, Defendant shall explain the discounts). Starting on January 3, 2020 and then on or before the 1st of each month thereafter, Plaintiff shall deposit with the Court an amount equal to the current amount of mortgage payment. Parties to meet and confer to determine this amount to be included in the Court Order. Hearing Required for Defendant to provide evidence and authority that BSI is a "depository institution chartered under state or federal law" within the meaning of CC 2924.18. Any such evidence and authority shall be provided to the Court and the Plaintiff by no later than 8:30 a.m. on January 3, 2020. Plaintiff to prepare order. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. VS. MIKAIL FUGAROV
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD

rulings.Case Title: COLUMBIA MISSION BAY LLC VS. KENDRA GILES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kendra Giles' Demurrer To Complaint For Unlawful Detainer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2020 line 6. Defendant Kendra Giles' Demurrer To Complaint For Unlawful Detainer SUSTAINED with leave to amend. First Amended Complaint is deemed filed as of December 19, 2019. Defendant shall have 5 days from January 3, 2020 to file a response to the First Amended Complaint. = (501/CFH)
